title: lobelia incisa
intro: Lobelia incisa, also known as Japanese lobelia or Japanese pukawai, is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is an annual herb native to Japan and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. Lobelia incisa contains lobeline, a compound that has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and spasmolytic properties. The plant can be prepared as an herbal remedy in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which have been traditionally used to relieve coughs, colds, and other respiratory problems, while also providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory health benefits.
